svg.text: 4-channel 12V LED strip dimmer (PWM low-side switching) with ESP32-WROOM-32 + HomeSpan

g.text: USB-side at top — only pins used in the project shown

svg.text: GND rail (common: 12V supply, buck output, ESP32, MOSFET sources, pull-downs)

g.text: • Topology (each channel): LED+ → +12V rail. LED− → MOSFET drain (D). MOSFET source (S) → GND. MOSFET gate (G) → junction node → R_gate → GPIO; junction node → R_pulldown → GND.
• R1–R4 (330Ω): series gate resistors limiting inrush current into the MOSFET gate capacitance during switching transitions (~10 mA peak from a 3.3V GPIO).
• R5–R8 (10kΩ): gate pull-downs holding each MOSFET off while the ESP32 is booting or resetting (GPIOs are high-impedance during this window), so the LEDs don't flash on at power-up.

• Wire crossings without dots: the four GPIO signal traces cross the +12V rail, and each gate trace crosses its own (and possibly other channels') MOSFET source-drop wire. No junction dot = no electrical connection.
